---
title: "LLM Visibility"
description: "What LLM visibility means, how it differs from search visibility, and how to measure it."
---

# LLM Visibility

**LLM visibility** is the degree to which a brand, product, or person appears accurately and favorably in responses generated by large language models — including ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Perplexity, and similar platforms.

It's the AI-era equivalent of search visibility. Where search visibility measures how often your brand appears in Google results for relevant queries, LLM visibility measures how often your brand appears in AI-generated responses to relevant questions.

## Why It's Different from Search Visibility

Search visibility is measurable with precision. Tools like Google Search Console, Ahrefs, and SEMrush give you ranking positions, impressions, and click data for specific keywords.

LLM visibility is harder to measure because AI platforms don't publish the equivalent of a ranking report. There's no "position 1" in a ChatGPT response. Your brand either appears or it doesn't, and the response varies by query phrasing, platform, and the model's current training and retrieval state.

This is why [AI brand score](/glossary/ai-brand-score) audits — running structured query tests across multiple platforms and recording results — are the primary diagnostic tool for LLM visibility today.

## What Determines LLM Visibility

LLM visibility is a function of several overlapping signals:

**Training data presence** — brands that appear frequently and accurately in the text large language models were trained on have stronger baseline visibility. This is influenced by press coverage, industry publications, and authoritative mentions over time.

**Retrieval performance** — for platforms using [RAG](/glossary/rag), LLM visibility at query time depends on how well your content performs in the [retrieval layer](/glossary/retrieval-layer). Strong [AEO](/glossary/aeo) practices directly improve this.

**Entity definition** — a well-defined brand [entity](/glossary/entity-optimization) gets represented more accurately and consistently. Vague or conflicting entity signals produce inconsistent or absent LLM visibility.

**Citation authority** — brands cited frequently by sources that AI systems trust develop stronger retrieval associations over time. This is the core premise of [citation architecture](/glossary/citation-architecture).

## LLM Visibility vs. AI Traffic

LLM visibility measures appearances — how often your brand shows up in AI responses. [AI traffic](/glossary/ai-traffic) measures clicks — how often users follow a link from an AI response to your site.

High LLM visibility with low AI traffic usually means your brand is being mentioned without being linked. High AI traffic with low broader LLM visibility usually means you're performing well on a narrow set of queries but not across your full category.

Both metrics matter. Neither tells the complete story alone.
